title: Luffy Gets Lost in Nanohana
description: On pages 1–4, this part focuses on luffy gets lost in nanohana, showing the key character choices and conflict behind this chapter beat.
scene: Luffy Gets Lost in Nanohana. Luffy runs into both Marines and his brother Ace in Nanohana, turning the Alabasta arrival into a chaotic reunion. The scene description focuses on what the reader needs to understand before the dialogue continues.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Trying to find Luffy in a city as large as Nanohana is going to be near-impossible!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: All we have to do is look in the noisiest part of town,

dialogue.speaker: Smoker
dialogue.text: And he'll be there for sure.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: If there's something to worry about,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: I wish he'd pay just a little more attention to that, especially in a country as large as Alabasta...!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: For now, we should go and eat something as well. We can think about all that stuff later.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: That ship can only be operated by the power of the Dora doru fruit...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: So he must be here in this country...

dialogue.speaker: Ace
dialogue.text: Did you hear? He's one of the Whitebeard pirates...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: That marine called Smoker's pretty famous too!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: What d'ya want me to do...?

dialogue.speaker: Tashigi
dialogue.text: What's that? I smell some- thing strange...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: ...I'm already on the hunt for another pirate right now,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: So I don't particularly care about taking your head...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: So just look the other way then.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: I'm afraid I can't do that.

summary: This part highlights luffy gets lost in nanohana and moves the chapter forward through its central emotional or plot turn.
